summary: Ukrainian forces used FPV drones to free captured soldiers by striking an armed Russian guard during an escort operation, with additional support from defense units. Russian forces deployed the "Yolki" interceptor drone, a 1.3 kg autonomous system capable of speeds up to 230 km/h and a 3 km interception range, to neutralize Ukrainian UAVs, with operators praising its ease of use and AI-driven targeting. The "Rubicon" Center reported destroying 100 Ukrainian targets, including personnel, fixed-wing UAVs, and UAV control centers, while Ukrainian soldiers survived a Russian FPV attack near Kostiantynivka, aided by drone detectors. Russian Ka-52 helicopter crew were eliminated after a crash landing near Nadiivka, with follow-up strikes targeting evacuation attempts.
